hypothesis: Augmenting a conservative D3-corrected uMLIP with a self-consistent, geometry-dependent charge model to capture long-range electrostatics will halve the median absolute deviation from DFT-optimized cell volumes and reduce by at least 30% the fraction of 50 ps NpT (300 K, 1 bar) simulations showing >10% volume drift for MOFs with open metal sites, compared to the same architecture without charges.
